speaker: QUEEN
text: I will not speak with her.

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: She is importunate,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Indeed distract; her mood will needs be pitied.

speaker: QUEEN
text: What would she have?

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: She speaks much of her father, says she hears

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: There’s tricks i’ th’ world, and hems, and beats her heart,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Spurns enviously at straws, speaks things in doubt

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: That carry but half sense. Her speech is nothing,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Yet the unshapèd use of it doth move

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: The hearers to collection. They aim at it

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: And botch the words up fit to their own thoughts;

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Which, as her winks and nods and gestures yield them,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Indeed would make one think there might be thought,

speaker: GENTLEMAN
text: Though nothing sure, yet much unhappily.

speaker: HORATIO
text: ’Twere good she were spoken with, for she may strew

speaker: HORATIO
text: Dangerous conjectures in ill-breeding minds.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Let her come in.

speaker: QUEEN
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              To my sick soul (as sin’s true nature is),

speaker: QUEEN
text: Each toy seems prologue to some great amiss.

speaker: QUEEN
text: So full of artless jealousy is guilt,

speaker: QUEEN
text: It spills itself in fearing to be spilt.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Where is the beauteous Majesty of Denmark?

speaker: QUEEN
text: How now, Ophelia?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: How should I your true love know

speaker: OPHELIA
text: From another one?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: By his cockle hat and staff

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And his sandal shoon.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alas, sweet lady, what imports this song?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Say you? Nay, pray you, mark.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He is dead and gone, lady,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He is dead and gone;

speaker: OPHELIA
text: At his head a grass-green turf,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: At his heels a stone.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Oh, ho!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Nay, but Ophelia—

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Pray you, mark.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: White his shroud as the mountain snow—

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alas, look here, my lord.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Larded all with sweet flowers;

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Which bewept to the ground did not go

speaker: OPHELIA
text: With true-love showers.

speaker: KING
text: How do you, pretty lady?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Well, God dild you. They say the owl was a

speaker: OPHELIA
text: baker’s daughter. Lord, we know what we are but

speaker: OPHELIA
text: know not what we may be. God be at your table.

speaker: KING
text: Conceit upon her father.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Pray let’s have no words of this, but when

speaker: OPHELIA
text: they ask you what it means, say you this:

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Tomorrow is Saint Valentine’s day,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: All in the morning betime,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And I a maid at your window,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: To be your Valentine.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Then up he rose and donned his clothes

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And dupped the chamber door,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Let in the maid, that out a maid

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Never departed more.

speaker: KING
text: Pretty Ophelia—

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Indeed, without an oath, I’ll make an end on ’t:

speaker: OPHELIA
text: By Gis and by Saint Charity,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Alack and fie for shame,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Young men will do ’t, if they come to ’t;

speaker: OPHELIA
text: By Cock, they are to blame.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Quoth she “Before you tumbled me,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: You promised me to wed.”

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He answers:

speaker: OPHELIA
text: “So would I ’a done, by yonder sun,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: An thou hadst not come to my bed.”

speaker: KING
text: How long hath she been thus?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: I hope all will be well. We must be patient,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: but I cannot choose but weep to think they would

speaker: OPHELIA
text: lay him i’ th’ cold ground. My brother shall know of

speaker: OPHELIA
text: it. And so I thank you for your good counsel. Come,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: my coach! Good night, ladies, good night, sweet

speaker: OPHELIA
text: ladies, good night, good night.

speaker: KING
text: Follow her close; give her good watch, I pray you.

speaker: KING
text: O, this is the poison of deep grief. It springs

speaker: KING
text: All from her father’s death, and now behold!

speaker: KING
text: O Gertrude, Gertrude,

speaker: KING
text: When sorrows come, they come not single spies,

speaker: KING
text: But in battalions: first, her father slain;

speaker: KING
text: Next, your son gone, and he most violent author

speaker: KING
text: Of his own just remove; the people muddied,

speaker: KING
text: Thick, and unwholesome in their thoughts and whispers

speaker: KING
text: For good Polonius’ death, and we have done but greenly

speaker: KING
text: In hugger-mugger to inter him; poor Ophelia

speaker: KING
text: Divided from herself and her fair judgment,

speaker: KING
text: Without the which we are pictures or mere beasts;

speaker: KING
text: Last, and as much containing as all these,

speaker: KING
text: Her brother is in secret come from France,

speaker: KING
text: Feeds on his wonder, keeps himself in clouds,

speaker: KING
text: And wants not buzzers to infect his ear

speaker: KING
text: With pestilent speeches of his father’s death,

speaker: KING
text: Wherein necessity, of matter beggared,

speaker: KING
text: Will nothing stick our person to arraign

speaker: KING
text: In ear and ear. O, my dear Gertrude, this,

speaker: KING
text: Like to a murd’ring piece, in many places

speaker: KING
text: Gives me superfluous death.

speaker: QUEEN
text: Alack, what noise is this?

speaker: KING
text: Attend!

speaker: KING
text: Where is my Switzers? Let them guard the door.

speaker: KING
text: What is the matter?

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Save yourself, my lord.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The ocean, overpeering of his list,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Eats not the flats with more impiteous haste

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Than young Laertes, in a riotous head,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: O’erbears your officers. The rabble call him “lord,”

speaker: MESSENGER
text: And, as the world were now but to begin,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Antiquity forgot, custom not known,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The ratifiers and props of every word,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: They cry “Choose we, Laertes shall be king!”

speaker: MESSENGER
text: Caps, hands, and tongues applaud it to the clouds,

speaker: MESSENGER
text: “Laertes shall be king! Laertes king!”

speaker: QUEEN
text: How cheerfully on the false trail they cry.

speaker: QUEEN
text: O, this is counter, you false Danish dogs!

speaker: KING
text: The doors are broke.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Where is this king?—Sirs, stand you all without.

speaker: ALL
text: No, let’s come in!

speaker: LAERTES
text: I pray you, give me leave.

speaker: ALL
text: We will, we will.

speaker: LAERTES
text:
I thank you. Keep the door.
                 
                Followers
                 
                exit
                .
               O, thou vile king,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Give me my father!

speaker: QUEEN
text: Calmly, good Laertes.

speaker: LAERTES
text: That drop of blood that’s calm proclaims me bastard,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Cries “cuckold” to my father, brands the harlot

speaker: LAERTES
text: Even here between the chaste unsmirchèd brow

speaker: LAERTES
text: Of my true mother.

speaker: KING
text: What is the cause, Laertes,

speaker: KING
text: That thy rebellion looks so giant-like?—

speaker: KING
text: Let him go, Gertrude. Do not fear our person.

speaker: KING
text: There’s such divinity doth hedge a king

speaker: KING
text: That treason can but peep to what it would,

speaker: KING
text: Acts little of his will.—Tell me, Laertes,

speaker: KING
text: Why thou art thus incensed.—Let him go, Gertrude.—

speaker: KING
text: Speak, man.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Where is my father?

speaker: KING
text: Dead.

speaker: QUEEN
text: But not by him.

speaker: KING
text: Let him demand his fill.

speaker: LAERTES
text: How came he dead? I’ll not be juggled with.

speaker: LAERTES
text: To hell, allegiance! Vows, to the blackest devil!

speaker: LAERTES
text: Conscience and grace, to the profoundest pit!

speaker: LAERTES
text: I dare damnation. To this point I stand,

speaker: LAERTES
text: That both the worlds I give to negligence,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Let come what comes, only I’ll be revenged

speaker: LAERTES
text: Most throughly for my father.

speaker: KING
text: Who shall stay you?

speaker: LAERTES
text: My will, not all the world.

speaker: LAERTES
text: And for my means, I’ll husband them so well

speaker: LAERTES
text: They shall go far with little.

speaker: KING
text: Good Laertes,

speaker: KING
text: If you desire to know the certainty

speaker: KING
text: Of your dear father, is ’t writ in your revenge

speaker: KING
text: That, swoopstake, you will draw both friend and foe,

speaker: KING
text: Winner and loser?

speaker: LAERTES
text: None but his enemies.

speaker: KING
text: Will you know them, then?

speaker: LAERTES
text: To his good friends thus wide I’ll ope my arms

speaker: LAERTES
text: And, like the kind life-rend’ring pelican,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Repast them with my blood.

speaker: KING
text: Why, now you speak

speaker: KING
text: Like a good child and a true gentleman.

speaker: KING
text: That I am guiltless of your father’s death

speaker: KING
text: And am most sensibly in grief for it,

speaker: KING
text: It shall as level to your judgment ’pear

speaker: KING
text: As day does to your eye.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text:
A
                 
                noise
                 
                within
                :
                 
              “Let her come in!”

speaker: LAERTES
text: How now, what noise is that?

speaker: LAERTES
text: O heat, dry up my brains! Tears seven times salt

speaker: LAERTES
text: Burn out the sense and virtue of mine eye!

speaker: LAERTES
text: By heaven, thy madness shall be paid with weight

speaker: LAERTES
text: Till our scale turn the beam! O rose of May,

speaker: LAERTES
text: Dear maid, kind sister, sweet Ophelia!

speaker: LAERTES
text: O heavens, is ’t possible a young maid’s wits

speaker: LAERTES
text: Should be as mortal as an old man’s life?

speaker: LAERTES
text: Nature is fine in love, and, where ’tis fine,

speaker: LAERTES
text: It sends some precious instance of itself

speaker: LAERTES
text: After the thing it loves.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: They bore him barefaced on the bier,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Hey non nonny, nonny, hey nonny,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And in his grave rained many a tear.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Fare you well, my dove.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Hadst thou thy wits and didst persuade revenge,

speaker: LAERTES
text: It could not move thus.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: You must sing “A-down a-down”—and you

speaker: OPHELIA
text: “Call him a-down-a.”—O, how the wheel becomes

speaker: OPHELIA
text: it! It is the false steward that stole his master’s

speaker: OPHELIA
text: daughter.

speaker: LAERTES
text: This nothing’s more than matter.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: There’s rosemary, that’s for remembrance.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Pray you, love, remember. And there is pansies,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: that’s for thoughts.

speaker: LAERTES
text: A document in madness: thoughts and remembrance

speaker: LAERTES
text: fitted.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: There’s fennel for you, and columbines.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: There’s rue for you, and here’s some for me; we

speaker: OPHELIA
text: may call it herb of grace o’ Sundays. You must wear

speaker: OPHELIA
text: your rue with a difference. There’s a daisy. I would

speaker: OPHELIA
text: give you some violets, but they withered all when

speaker: OPHELIA
text: my father died. They say he made a good end.

speaker: OPHELIA
text:
Sings
                .
                 
              For bonny sweet Robin is all my joy.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Thought and afflictions, passion, hell itself

speaker: LAERTES
text: She turns to favor and to prettiness.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And will he not come again?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And will he not come again?

speaker: OPHELIA
text: No, no, he is dead.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: Go to thy deathbed.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He never will come again.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: His beard was as white as snow,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: All flaxen was his poll.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: He is gone, he is gone,

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And we cast away moan.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: God ’a mercy on his soul.

speaker: OPHELIA
text: And of all Christians’ souls, I pray God. God be wi’

speaker: OPHELIA
text: you.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Do you see this, O God?

speaker: KING
text: Laertes, I must commune with your grief,

speaker: KING
text: Or you deny me right. Go but apart,

speaker: KING
text: Make choice of whom your wisest friends you will,

speaker: KING
text: And they shall hear and judge ’twixt you and me.

speaker: KING
text: If by direct or by collateral hand

speaker: KING
text: They find us touched, we will our kingdom give,

speaker: KING
text: Our crown, our life, and all that we call ours,

speaker: KING
text: To you in satisfaction; but if not,

speaker: KING
text: Be you content to lend your patience to us,

speaker: KING
text: And we shall jointly labor with your soul

speaker: KING
text: To give it due content.

speaker: LAERTES
text: Let this be so.

speaker: LAERTES
text: His means of death, his obscure funeral

speaker: LAERTES
text: (No trophy, sword, nor hatchment o’er his bones,

speaker: LAERTES
text: No noble rite nor formal ostentation)

speaker: LAERTES
text: Cry to be heard, as ’twere from heaven to earth,

speaker: LAERTES
text: That I must call ’t in question.

speaker: KING
text: So you shall,

speaker: KING
text: And where th’ offense is, let the great ax fall.

speaker: KING
text: I pray you, go with me.
